title: Fumana ferruginea
taxonomy: Fumana ferruginea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a member of the genus Fumana, which is native to the Mediterranean region. Fumana ferruginea is a shrubby perennial plant that produces yellow flowers and has a reddish-brown color. This plant is also known as rusty fumana or rusty rockrose, and it is commonly found in dry, rocky areas. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but Fumana ferruginea is generally considered to be a member of the family Cistaceae.
uses: Fumana ferruginea, also known as rusty cliff-fleabane, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits. In some regions, the plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various skin conditions. Its leaves and flowers are sometimes used in herbal teas to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, the plant is thought to possess antimicrobial properties, which may help protect against infections. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Fumana ferruginea as a medicinal agent.
